item.title: Completion notification when auto session ends or circuit-breaker fires
item.intent: When auto done finishes — whether by draining the backlog, hitting the circuit breaker, or reaching --limit — the user gets an immediate terminal bell and optional system notification so they know the session is over without watching the terminal.
item.description: Users run auto done on large backlogs that take 30-60+ minutes. Currently the session ends silently: green text appears but the user only sees it if they happen to be watching. A terminal bell (tput bel) is zero-config and works in any terminal. A richer system notification (osascript on macOS, notify-send on Linux) is opt-in via config. Both fire on clean completion and on circuit-breaker failure, with different messages.
steps: On session end (drained, circuit-breaker, or --limit reached), call tput bel to send a terminal bell — no config required, works everywhere
Add notifications.system config key (default: false); when true, detect platform and call osascript (macOS) or notify-send (Linux) with summary message (N items completed, M failed)
Add AISHORE_NOTIFY env var override (true/false) consistent with other env overrides
Bell and system notification fire on both clean exit and circuit-breaker failure, with distinct messages ('Sprint session complete' vs 'Sprint session stopped: N consecutive failures')
Update docs/CONFIGURATION.md with notifications.system key and AISHORE_NOTIFY env var
acceptanceCriteria: After auto done drains the backlog, a terminal bell sounds regardless of config
With notifications.system: true, a system notification appears on macOS (osascript) and Linux (notify-send) at session end
Circuit-breaker exit also triggers bell and system notification (if enabled) with a failure message
AISHORE_NOTIFY=false suppresses system notification even when config enables it
